title: Add M4 borrowed-shadow crisis chain
summary: Add the borrowed-shadow anomaly rule graph and explicit crisis state machine for the focused lamp and Chronicle rule-discovery scenario.
acceptance: Borrowed-shadow activation depends on lamp gap state and Chronicle identity evidence basis from approved owner stores
Crisis progress uses an explicit serializable state machine with activation trace escalation resolution and terminal reason fields
The chain creates low-risk evidence before irreversible harm and supports at least one non-combat containment or negotiation resolution
Crisis candidate selection is bounded and versioned and cannot scan all lamps evidence obligations residents or map cells in normal ticks
Focused tests cover activation prevention escalation resolution failure terminal cleanup structured reasons save-shaped state and no coroutine Promise closure or UI-local progress

history.details: Created by WM-0061 M4 planning DAG; remains proposed until dependencies are done.

Implemented borrowed-shadow crisis owner store with bounded activation candidates, explicit state machine progress, low-risk evidence traces, non-combat containment/negotiation resolution, focused tests, docs and performance notes.

changes_requested: High: resolveCrisis can terminally resolve directly from ACTIVATED with no low-risk evidence or escalation, violating the ordered borrowed-shadow chain; High: failCrisis accepts TERMINAL_HARM from ACTIVATED before any low-risk evidence, allowing irreversible harm before evidence; High: activation basis validation accepts negative/non-uint32 version fields, negative identityIndependentClassCount, and oversized identitySupportTier, causing typed-array wrap/truncation in owner state. Required checks pass but focused regressions are missing.

Repaired borrowed-shadow crisis ordering and validation: resolution now requires ESCALATED state, irreversible HARM requires prior low-risk evidence, activation basis rejects typed-array wrapping inputs, added focused regressions; required checks and quality pass.

changes_requested: High: TERMINAL_HARM still bypasses the ordered crisis chain after low-risk evidence; failCrisis only checks lowRiskEvidenceCount and can move TRACE directly to FAILED/HARM with escalationTick unset and escalationLevel 0. WM-0065 docs/spec define activated -> trace -> escalated -> resolved|failed, so irreversible harm should require ESCALATED state. Prior resolution-before-escalation and typed-array validation repairs are otherwise verified; required checks pass.

Repaired second WM-0065 review finding: TERMINAL_HARM now requires ESCALATED state, HARM from TRACE after low-risk evidence is rejected without state/count changes, HARM after evidence plus escalation succeeds; required checks and quality pass.

verified: No blocking findings. Re-reviewed current WM-0065 worktree including untracked crisis source/tests; HARM now requires ESCALATED state, prior resolution/HARM/typed-array validation blockers are fixed, acceptance evidence and scope boundaries are satisfied. Required checks and pnpm quality pass.
Verified borrowed-shadow crisis owner surface accepted after reviewer-requested ordering and validation repairs.

objective: Implement one explicit anomaly/crisis chain that proves the M4 lamp plus Chronicle rule without broad anomaly catalog or M5 content.
forbiddenPaths: story director implementation beyond passive pressure counters
M5 anomaly roster faction campaign or content framework
public save format or schema changes
hidden shared state coroutine Promise closure or UI-local crisis progress
benchmarkImpact: Adds crisis transition active/recovered crisis terminal-reason and low-risk evidence counters; baseline updates wait for WM-0070.
reviewRouting: Simulation-engineer completes to reviewer; systems-architect must be consulted before changing crisis ownership, save-shaped state, or anomaly rule semantics.
rollbackModel: Revert borrowed-shadow anomaly/crisis stores, tests, metrics and docs; preserve lamp Chronicle obligation and M0-M3 behavior.
ownerGate: Keep crisis progress explicit and serializable
Require lamp and evidence basis for activation and escalation
Provide non-combat resolution and structured accident explanation
Block on director overreach public protocol save schema dependency UI authority broad combat M5 or hidden-state scope

sparkEligibility.reason: Anomaly/crisis state-machine work is high-risk deterministic simulation design.

review.summary: No blocking findings. Re-reviewed current WM-0065 worktree including untracked crisis source/tests; HARM now requires ESCALATED state, prior resolution/HARM/typed-array validation blockers are fixed, acceptance evidence and scope boundaries are satisfied. Required checks and pnpm quality pass.

integration.summary: Verified borrowed-shadow crisis owner surface accepted after reviewer-requested ordering and validation repairs.
